titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Fredric R. Mann Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Michelle Peralta

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.
This collection contains 12 published digital objects.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Saturday August 28, 2021 at 00:50

descriptivenote.p: Materials are in English, Hebrew, French, and Spanish.

did.abstract:
The Fredric R. Mann papers primarily document Mann's charitable and philanthropic efforts, particularly of the arts and music, his involvement in the Jewish community and relations between Philadelphia and Israel. The papers also document Mann's governmental work in a variety of posts for the City of Philadelphia, the State of Pennsylvania, and the United States.
Of particular note are the materials related to the history and development of the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra (formerly the Palestine Orchestra), the Philadelphia Orchestra and the Robin Hood Dell West/Fredric R. Mann Music Center in Fairmount Park, Philadelphia, as well as Mann's post as the City Representative for Philadelphia. The correspondence series contains letters from international musicians, public figures, and heads of state.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The papers are organized in six series: I. Biographical materials, 1910-1997. II. Topical files, 1917-1999. III. Correspondence, 1927-1987. IV. Photographs and photo albums, 1920-1986. V. Scrapbooks 1935-1980. VI. Clippings, Circa 1920-circa 1999.
Digital materials have been integrated into the appropriate series based on content.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers primarily document Mann's charitable and philanthropic efforts, particularly of the arts and music, his involvement in the Jewish community and relations between Philadelphia and Israel. The papers also document Mann's governmental work in a variety of posts for the City of Philadelphia, the State of Pennsylvania, and the United States.

Of particular note are the materials related to the history and development of the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra (formerly the Palestine Orchestra), the Philadelphia Orchestra and the Robin Hood Dell West/Fredric R. Mann Music Center in Fairmount Park, Philadelphia, as well as Mann's post as the City Representative for Philadelphia. The correspondence series contains letters from international musicians, public figures, and heads of state.

Materials include correspondence, photographs, newspaper clippings and articles, and audiovisual materials. Digital materials were recorded in 1982 and 1997 onto audiocassette tapes. These tapes were subsequently digitized in 2019. Dates for these files were determined based on the date the original tapes were recorded.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Fredric Rand Mann (1903-1987) was born in Gromel, Russia and immigrated to the United States at a young age with his family settling in New Haven, Connecticut. As a child, he returned to Russia to study concert piano under Arthur Freidheim. However, a car accident in his teens prevented Mann from pursuing a professional career as a musician. He was admitted to Yale College class of 1923, but withdrew after his sophomore year. Mann relocated to Philadelphia and attended the Wharton School of Business at the University of Pennsylvania.

Mann started the Seaboard Container Corporation, a corrugated cardboard company, and served as its president until 1953 when it merged with the National Container Corporation. He served as its president from 1954 to 1956, when it was sold. Mann went on to establish another box company, the Industrial Container Corporation of Newark, New Jersey, and served as its president as well as the president of another box comapny, MannKraft Corporation.

Civically engaged, Mann has held several posts with the City of Philadelphia, such as the City Representative and Director of Commerce (1956-1966), Recreation Commissioner (1952-1955), and the President of the Fairmount Park Commission (1951-1986). On the state level, Mann was a member of the Governor of Pennsylvania's Advisory Council on Recreation and a commisioner of the Delaware River Port Authority. Federally, Mann was the American Ambassador to Barbados and United States Special Representative to the Associated States of the West Indies (1967-1969).

As a patron of the arts, Mann was the President of Robin Hood Dell Concerts, which held a summer concert series featuring the Philadelphia Orchestra at the Robin Hood Dell. In 1967 a new concert hall was created in Fairmount Park, the Robin Hood Dell West, which was renamed the Fredric R. Mann Music Center in 1969. Mann was a member of several music-related boards include the Philadelphia Orchestra Association, the New York Philharmonic, and Carnegie Hall.

Internationally, Mann was heavily involved with the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra, raising funds with the American-Israeli Cultural Foundation for the develoption of a concert hall in Tel-Aviv, Israel. In 1957 the Fredric R. Mann Auditorium was dedicated in his honor.
Mann passed away in Florida on February 26, 1987 at 83 years old.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 154, Fredric R. Mann Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by the donor, Alexis Denny Kaufmann, for unpublished materials. After the lifetime of Alexis Denny Kaufmann, copyright passes to Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.
As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Contact the Music library at musicspecialcollections@yale.edu to request access.

corpname.part: Robin Hood Dell Orchestra of Philadelphia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains biographical and personal materials related to Mann's life, including educational documents, certificates of achievements and honors, as well as memorials and obituaries.

Digital materials included in this series were recorded in 1982 and 1997 onto audiocassette tapes. These tapes were subsequently digitized in 2019. Dates for these files were determined based on the date the original tapes were recorded.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged chronologically by year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Mann biographical sketches and profiles, interview transcripts, and magazine and newspaper clippings. Includes transcript of an interview with Walter M. Phillips and material related to him as a pianst.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials may be attributed to Fredric Manevetz.

did.unittitle: Adams, John S. 5000 Musical Terms: A Complete Dictonary
Piano performances and primary school activities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains certifcates, awards, honorary degrees, and official resolutions regarding honors bestowed upon Mann. Also includes sheet music dedicated to Mann.

did.unittitle: Mann's piano performance of Chopin at the 25th anniversary of the Fredric R. Mann Auditorium

descriptivenote.p: [Aviary] Mann's piano performance of Chopin at the 25th anniversary of the Fredric R. Mann Auditorium, 1982

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material was transfered to the Music Library in 2020 for preservation and access. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Original audio was recorded in 1982 onto audiocassette tapes. These tapes were subsequently digitized in 2019. Dates for these files were determined based on the date the originals were recorded.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Streaming access is provided via Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: 1) Eyewitness News (KYW-TV); 2) ABC Action News (WPVI-TV); and 3) "80th birthday celebration for Mann" (WCAU-TV) news segments.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

did.unittitle: TV news segments (15) on the death and funeral of Fredric R. Mann

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains pamphlets and musical programs dedicated to Mann. Also includes funeral book.

did.unittitle: Recorded interviews about Fredric R. Mann

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Interviews were conducted in 1997 by Alexis Denny Kaufmann. Original audio was recorded onto audiocassette tapes. These tapes were subsequently digitized in 2019. Dates for these files were determined based on the date the originals were recorded.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This material was transfered to the Music Library in 2020 for preservation and access. Additional information about actions taken may be available at the repository.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Streaming access is provided via Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Streaming access is provided via Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Streaming access is provided via Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary.

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Streaming access is provided via Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Streaming access is provided via Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original digital materials may not be used. Researchers must consult access copies. Streaming access is provided via Yale University Library's AV access system, Aviary.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes materials related to Mann's governmental and philantrophic work, as well as Mann's broad interest in music and involvement in Jewish community concerns. Materials primarily represented include correspondence, articles and publications, and event programs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged alphabetically by topic and chronologically therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Formerly called the American Fund for Israeli Institutions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes programs for the inaugural dinner, a scholarship anniversary program, and tribute to Arturo Toscanini.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains tribute and profile on Mann.

did.unittitle: Fredric R. Mann Cultural and Civic Center program design

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also: Fredric R. Mann Auditorium.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, foundation minutes, and press releases pertaining to dispute with Reuven Dafni.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Tarbut: A publication of the America-Israel Cultural Foundation .

title.part: Tarbut: A Publication of the America-Israel Foundation

did.unittitle: American Ambassador to Barbados and United States Special Representative to the Associated States of the West Indies
American Embassy, Barbados, West Indies - Brief

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally in binders. Includes 1968 issue of the Philadelphia Magazine .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters and telegrams with other United States agencies, invitations, and copies of addresses delivered, letters and cards from John S. Bennett, British High Commissioner, and Grace Kelly and Prince Rainier III, and Mann's resignation letter to President Nixon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and Mann press release regarding the rumor of him being appointed as the Ambassador to Israel by John F. Kennedy.

did.unittitle: American Society for Technion, Women's Division

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes general correspondence for the Israel Institue of Technology.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence related to a model of the Israeli pavilion on exhibition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, presentations, articles and press releases related to Mann's advocacy for civic arts and culture programs. Contains materials for the 1965 conference, "Municipality and the Arts," where he acted as the City of Philadelphia representative; 1963 TV interview (WHYY-TV) fact sheet regarding support of culture and performing arts in Philadelphia; and materials related to the U.S. House of Representatives Committee on Education and Labor, Select Subcommittee of Education, as well as the Senate Committee on Labor and Public Welfare, Special Subcomittee on the Arts. Includes copies of Mann's "Statement on Conditions Affecting the Income and Employment of Performing Artists in America."

did.unittitle: Building and Loan Association of Philadelphia account books

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also signed by President Truman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily contains correspondence related to the Mann's various contributions to Jewish organizations and political campaigns. Also contains 1964 issue of The Illustrated London News featuring a section on "Survey of the Modern State of Israel."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence form Rabbi Richard F. Steinbrink.

did.unittitle: Congressional Record, Proceedings and Debates of the 96th Congress

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remarks delivered to the U.S. House of Representatives regarding Mann's accomplishments in Philadelphia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, annual reports, and handbook pertaining to Mann's appointment as commissioner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials for the Philadelphia and national democratic committees.

did.unittitle: "A Safari Through Fairmount Park" article from Greater Philadelphia: The Magazine for Executives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily correspondence related to the Friars Club, Fellowship Commission, as well as Thomas M. Foglietta and translator Mark Fradkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a hat and an issue of Slipper Writes , another publication of the Golden Slipper Square Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The publication of the Golden Slipper Square Club.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Program contains Mann's annotations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known as the Hebrew Sheltering and Immigrant Aid Society.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains brochures on the merge of Mann Kraft and St. Joe Paper Company.

did.unittitle: International Association of Chiefs of Police

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs with David Ben-Gurion, and possibly Yitzhak Ben-Zvi and Yitzhak Rabin. Also contains a correspondence with Abba Eban, Shimon Peres, and Teddy Kollek and other Israeli heads of state.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to events such as correspondence, programs, and ephemera. Also includes newsletters and photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, clippings, and photographs related to the museum inauguration and the Diorite head of Gudea.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Palestine Orchestra is the predecessor to the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains programs and orchestra history.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies from letters and articles kept in the "Huberman Archives" in the Central Music Library in Israel, created on the occasion of the beginning of the 31st season of the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra.

did.unittitle: "A Celebration for the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Presented by the American Fund for Israel Institutions, on the occasion of the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra's first visit to the United States. The orchestra was to tour forty cities, holding fifty-five concerts in the United States and Canada between January 7 and March 17, 1951. Also includes the roster of guests for the inaugural dinner held on January 8, 1951.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1961 postage stamp.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: with Lorin Maazel conducting the Israel Philharmonic Orchesta and signed by Henry Haftel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings written in Hebrew.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains images of the Palestine Symphony Orchestra around 1936-1937.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes publications commemorating the Orchestra's 30 and 40-year history, booklets from the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra Association, and an issue of Philharmonic Hall featuring the Orchestra.

did.unittitle: Proposal for the preservation of newspaper clippings in the archives of the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra
Graphic program for renovation and extention

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Created by Yacov Rechter-Amnon Rechter, architects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains remarks given at Independence Hall, materials related to the 1964 Presidential inaugural committee for Johnson, as well as materials related to Johnson's funeral. Also includes signed Johnson family photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and clippings related to Kapell's performances and death. Concert programs, an art sketch, and a photograph are also included.

scopecontent.head: Includes materials related to a Pablo Casals concert.
scopecontent.p: Contains mostly invitations, programs, and clippings related to Kennedy's Presidential inauguration, visits to Philadelphia, and memorial.

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John F. and Johnson, Lyndon B. - Inaugural activities
Kennedy, John F., Center for the Peforming Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known as the National Cultural Center.

did.unittitle: The Burden and the Glory by John F. Kennedy, signed by Jacqueline Kennedy
Signed photograph to Silvia E. Mann

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials primarily document the construction and dedication of the Fredric R. Mann Auditorium. See also: Topical Files - Tel Aviv Cultural Center (T.A.C.C.) for early planning documents and financial correspondence; and Topica Files - Israel Philharmonic Orchestra (IPO) for music programs and events held at the auditorium.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters and telegrams related to the construction and associated financials for the auditorium. Primary correspondendents include the American Fund for Israel Institutions, America-Israel Cultural Foundation, and the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra. See also: Topic Files - Tel Aviv Cultural Center (T.A.C.C.).

did.unittitle: Fredric R. Mann Auditorium inauguration concert and dedication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, clippings, and photographs related to the 1957 inauguration of the Fredric R. Mann auditorium. Includes photo proofs and signed pamphlet of Arthur Rubinstein, Leonard Bernstein, Gregory Piatigorsky, and Isaac Stern, the conductor and soloists of the gala opening. Also contains a booklet about the Palestine Orchestra and statements and resolutions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also:"Israel Philharmonic Orchestra" for additional photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newspaper clippings primarily related to the dedication and opening of the auditorium, and various publications.

did.unittitle: "Memories in Sound: The Story of the Israel Philharmonic and the Mann Auditorium" by Alex Maimon

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings written in Hebrew.

did.unittitle: "30 Years of Culture: Festive Events on the 30th Anniversary of the Inauguration of the Fredric R. Mann Auditorium" program and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials for Robin Hood Dell West. See also: Topical Files - Robin Hood Dell and Topical Files - the Philadelphia Orchestra for related materials.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

did.unittitle: "The Mann Music Center Kicks Off Its 54th Season" ABC Action News (WPVI-TV)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known as the Westmoreland Center. Contains materials related to the center's dedication, including correspondence, articles, photographs, and ephemera.

did.unittitle: Mann, Fredric R., Mr. and Mrs., scholarship

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. All copies are provided as digital files. Researchers should consult the ordering reproductions information on the Music library website or contact: musicspecialcollections@yale.edu.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials related to foundation and school, as well as a letter to Silvia E. Mann.

did.unittitle: Monaco, Prince Rainier III and Princess Grace

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains early business plans of the National Container Association, business correspondence, as well as congratulatory letters upon Mann's appointment as a National Container Corporation executive. Also includes a directory of the National Container Corporation and its subsidiaries, a study conducted by EBASCO Services Incorporated, and annual reports from 1953 and 1954.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes newsletters from the Curtis instutite of Music (1975), Intermezzo : The magazine of Carnegie Hall (1977), and Quarternotes , the newletter for the New York Philharmonic (1980) with a feature on Isaac Stern.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains memos, correspondence, programs, and the 1982 annual report. Includes photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence related to Mann's acquisition and selling of the property at 6340 Wayne Avenue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, clippings, and report by Mann detailing the highlights of the Recreation Department from 1952 to 1955.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of Recreation , the magazine of the National Recreation Association and the feature titled "The Philadelphia Recreation Story" written by Fredric R. Mann.

did.unittitle: City Representative and Director of Commerce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials pertianing to Mann's appointment, inauguration, and resignation. Also contains correspondence related to salary raises, a resolution declared by the City of Philadelphia in Mann's honor for civil service, and newspaper clippings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials published by the office of the City Representative: "The Road Ahead," "The Song of Philadelphia," and "The New Philadelphia."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the feature "There's a little bit of Wausau in the new Philadelphia story" that was published in issues of Business Week , Time , and Newsweek .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials for official visits from heads of state, military, and scientific representativies to Philadelphia. Includes materials pertaining to visits from Carlos Julio Arosemena Monroy, President of Ecuador, Robert E. Briscoe; and trade specialists from Russia. Also contains publications commemorating the visits of Israel's Prime Minister Eshkol and Chile's President Alessandri visits to the United States.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes memberships rosters, correspondence, minutes, and press releases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "The Philadelphia Orchestra Story" and a publication from the Soviet Union written in Russian.

did.unittitle: Bach, Beethoven, and Bureaucracy: The Case of the Philadelphia Orchestra by Edward Arian

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains playbills and stagebills primarily of the Lincoln Center for the Performing Arts and Carnegie Hall. Also includes programs for performances by Sergei Rachmaninoff, Ignacy Jan Paderewski, Jose Kahan, Arthur Rubinstein, Isaac Stern, as well as programs from the Philadelphia Lyric Opera Company, the Philharmonic Society of New York/ New York Philharmonic, and the Royal Albert Hall.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documents the Robin Hood Dell and the Robin Hood Dell Concerts through 1976 when a new concert facility was built in Fairmount Park that was called Robin Hood Dell West. In 1979, Robin Hood Dell West was renamed the Fredric R. Mann Music Center. See also Topcial Files - Fredric R. Mann Music Center for materials related for Robin Hood Dell West; and Topical Files- The Philadelphia Orchestra for music programs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Words and music by William Benson Richter and dedicated to Mann.

did.unittitle: Roosevelt, Franklin D. and Harry S. Truman - President and Vice President inauguration

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, clippings, invitations and programs, as well as materials related to Rubenstein's funeral. Also contains photograph with Mann.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes new plant plans, publications, annual reports, as well as articles and corresporespondence related to the merge with the National Container Corporation.

did.unittitle: Signed sheet music for Silvia E. Mann

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, planning documents, and programs for the National Recreation Association, the Marian Anderson Center dedication, the American Municipal Congress, Dilworth -Blanc campaign events, the Philadelphia mayor's non-partisan register and voting events, and the Recreation supervisors dinner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, planning documents, and programs for the National Recreation Association, Robert Crawford bon voyage party, the Leader Woodland dinner, the Loyal Leagure Philanthropy, and a luncheon for Prince Rainier and Princess Grace of Monaco.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials for a Chaim Levanon luncheon, Robert Briscoe visit, and a Fellowship House dinner.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials for the Mann-Denny wedding, the Dilworth committee luncheon, and dinners for Ravidin and Singing City.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials for the Israel Philharmonic Orchestra in Philadelphia, a Tau Epsilon Phi reunion, a television dinner for the National Cultural Center, the Baldwin School/Piccaso tour, and the St. Agnes hospitality ball.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Mann's comments for the Today Magazine article "Philadelphia Remembered."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, invitations to the 1953 founders' day dinner honoring Mann as Tau Epsilon Phi "Man of the Year," and issues of The Plume .

did.unittitle: President and Vice President (Alben W. Barkley) inauguration

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, clippings, and planning documents related to the dedication ceremony and Truman's 70th birthday dinner fundraiser.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes guest lists and agenda.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes lists of contributors, records of expenses, and ledgers of donations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes invitation, program, and photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a certificate to Mann for contributions to library and a photograph of the library under constructions.

did.unittitle: United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization (UNESCO) delegation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes materials related to the Hillel Foundation and the 1948 consecreation of the Fredric R. Mann Chapel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from Chaim Weizmann and Meyer Weisgal.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Mann's speech.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes thank you letters, tickets, and report.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, issues of the Yale Alumni Magazine and Journal , and the 1978 class book of the Class of 1923.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in this series include letters both received and sent by Mann.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged by last name or name of organization, and chronologically therein.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings in Spanish.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily correspondence related to the Green Valley Country Club, Golden Door, the Graduate Hospital, and various politicians and officials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains correspondence from spouses Frances Spielberg and Florence Vidor, as well as son Joseph (Jay) Heifetz.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters from Bonislaw to Eugene Ormandy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes signed photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter needs conservation.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photograph of Iturbi.

did.unittitle: Johnson, Lyndon B. and Lady Bird (Claudia Alta)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from John F. Kennedy, Robert Kennedy, Edward Kennedy.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters related to William Kapell.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence related to the United Jewish Appeal; Union League of Philadelphia; and United Nations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains primarily letters from Isabelle Vengerova.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See also: the Weizmann Institute of Science under Series II. Topical files, 1917-1999.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged chronologically by year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains personal and family photographs. Includes a photographs of Mann with Nikita Krushchev, and Harry S. Truman. Also contains photographs documenting receiving an honorary degree from Springfield College in 1969; a trip to Havana.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily comprised of photographs of musicians, dancers, composers, and conductors. Also included are photographs with politicians and religious leaders.

did.unittitle: City Representative and Director of Commerce, City of Philadelphia - Photo albums
Independence Hall, Philadelphia, photograph by Madill, R.G.
Robin Hood Dell Concerts, Board of Directors
Association of Israel Journalists - Photo album

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs from Kiev.

did.unittitle: Delaware River Port Authority of Pennsylvania and New Jersey, Commissioners
Mann arrives at Bridgetown to assume his duties as United States Ambassador to Barbados
Mann presents check to Youth Town, Bridgetown; Mann presents a "liberty bown" to Prime Minister Errol Barrow
Mann presents credentials to Barbados' Govenor-General, Sir Winston Scott
Mann pays official call to the Prime Minister of Barbados, Mr. Errol Barrow
Mann bringing greetings from the United States youth to the youth of Barbados during a youth rally
Fredric R. Mann and Sylvia Mann as honored guests of the Counselor of Embassy, Charles P. Torrey
Mann with Mary Gay on her 100th birthday
Mann presents credentials to the Premier of Grenada, Eric Gairy
Mann signs investment guarantee agreement with Premier Eric Gairy
Mann and Deputy Premier of St. Lucia, the Hon. Hunter J. Francois, sign the investment guarantee agreement
Mann presents a copy of a resolution extending greetings on Pan American Day to the Speaker of Barbados House of Assembly, J.E.T. Brancker
Mann presents credentials to Premier Edward O. LeBlanc of Dominica
German Ambassador Michael Jovy pays a courtesy call to Mann
French Ambassador Paul Le Mintier de Lehelec pays a courtesy call to Mann
Mann accompanies Captain Alan McNaughton on a courtesy call to Govenor-General Sir Winston Scott
Mann at the Queen's birthday parade, Garrison Savannah
Mann presents credentials to the Premier of St. Christopher-Nevis-Anguilla, Robert L. Bradshaw
U.S. Ambassador Milton Barall visits Govenor-General Sir Winston Scott and Prime Minister Errol Barrow with Mann
Mann and Prime Minister Errol Barrow signing an investment guarantee agreement
Mann wih British High Commissioner to Barbados, John Bennett
Mann presenting prizes at the Barbados Show Jumping Federation's annual international show
Mann with Captain D.A. York, Officer Frank Hallissey, and Governor-General Sir Winston Scott
Mann presenting credentials to Premier of St. Lucia, John Compton, and calling on Governor Sir Frederick Clarke
Mann with Stephen C. Yao, Nationalist China's Ambassador to Barbados
Fredric R. Mann at the piano

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged chronologically by year.

did.unittitle: Hebrew Sheltering and Immigrant Aid Society (HIAS)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known ans the Hebrew Immigrant Aid and Society (HIAS). Contains materials in Hebrew.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the scrapbook is restricted due to fragility.

did.unittitle: Bonds of Israel Government tribute dinner
Loyal League of Philanthropies testimonial dinner
Fredric R. Mann Auditorium dedication story

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the scrapbook is restricted due to fragility.

did.unittitle: Resignation, City Representative and Directory of Commerce

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Details the 50th anniversary of the Robin Hood Dell concerts, the name change from Robin Hood Dell West to the Fredric R. Mann Music Center, and the first full season of the Philadelphia Orchestra at the Mann Music Center.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The series is arranged chronologically by year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in Hebrew.

did.unittitle: Jack Benny concert clippings and ephemera
